chapter: # Course 00: on boarding for AI Accelarotor
title: # Course 00: on boarding for AI Accelarotor
text:
# Course 00: on boarding for AI Accelarotor

---

## A. Overview — What the AI Career Accelerator Is

- its a full AI Engineer path with deeper, industry-level skills.  
- Covers ML → DL → GenAI with real deployment, optimization, and architecture work.  
- Focuses heavily on building a job-ready portfolio for high-paying AI roles.  
- Trains you to operate as:
  - AI Engineer  
  - Solutions Architect  
  - Automation Specialist  

---

## B. Core Program Components

### 1. AI Fundamentals
- Machine Learning foundations: supervised, unsupervised learning  
- Evaluation metrics and model assessment  
- Deep Learning: CNNs, RNNs, Transformers  
- Generative AI: LLMs, diffusion models, RAG systems  

---

title: # Course 00: on boarding for AI Accelarotor
text:
---

### 2. Advanced MLOps
- Model packaging, deployment, scaling  
- CI/CD pipelines for AI systems  
- Monitoring drift, performance, reliability in production  

---

### 3. Multi-Modality
- Building systems combining text, image, audio  
- Agent pipelines using multiple data types  

---

### 4. Open-Source Mastery
- When to go offline (privacy, compliance, restrictions)  
- Running models locally using PyTorch / TensorFlow + CUDA  
- GPU execution using `.to("cuda")`  
- Contributing to open-source AI ecosystems  

---

### 5. Deploy Models Anywhere
- Cloud: AWS / Azure / GCP  
- On-premise deployments  
- Local GPU setups  
- Platforms: Render, Railway, Supabase  
- Decision factors: cost, scale, latency  

---

title: # Course 00: on boarding for AI Accelarotor
text:
---

### 6. Fine-Tuning & Optimization
- Working with LLaMA, CLIP, Whisper, vision models  
- LoRA fine-tuning  
- Quantization and pruning  
- GPU optimization for performance and cost  
- Offline AI lab setups  

---

### 7. Automation Projects
- Workflow automation replacing business tasks  
- Tools: APIs, webhooks, CRMs, Zapier, Make  
- Building full AI-powered automation systems  

---

### 8. Solutions Architecture
- Designing full AI system architectures  
- Breaking systems into:
  - ingestion  
  - processing  
  - models  
  - APIs  
  - UI  
  - logging  
- Containerization (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes)  
- Transitioning to senior/lead roles  

---

title: # Course 00: on boarding for AI Accelarotor
text:
---

### 9. Career & Portfolio Building
- LinkedIn positioning for authority  
- GitHub optimization:
  - clean repositories  
  - structured documentation  
  - pinned projects  
- Portfolio with real-world impact projects  
- Resume/CV tailored for AI roles  
- Content strategy for visibility and recruitment  

---

## C. Program In-Depth — Detailed Breakdown

### 1. Where to Deploy
- Cloud: AWS, Azure, GCP, DigitalOcean  
- Lightweight platforms: Render, Supabase, Railway  
- Decision factors:
  - scale  
  - traffic  
  - latency  
  - cost  

---

### 2. Multi-Modality in Practice
- Systems combining text + image understanding  
- Example: AI agent that processes documents, screenshots, and instructions  

---

title: # Course 00: on boarding for AI Accelarotor
text:
---

### 3. Open-Source Offline Mastery
- When to use offline AI:
  - privacy constraints  
  - legal restrictions  
  - no internet environments  

- Setup:
  - install PyTorch, CUDA, transformers  
  - load models locally  

- GPU usage:
  - accelerates matrix operations in deep learning  
  - `.to("cuda")` moves models to GPU  

- Offline fine-tuning:
  - LoRA or full model training on local hardware  

---

### 4. Deployment Strategy
- On-prem client systems → deploy locally  
- Data center clients → Kubernetes / compute clusters  
- No infrastructure → cloud deployment (EC2, Vertex AI, Azure ML)  

---

title: # Course 00: on boarding for AI Accelarotor
text:
---

### 5. Fine-Tuning & Optimization (Deep Dive)
- Start with pre-trained models → adapt to domain  
- LoRA for efficient training  
- Quantization for faster inference and lower memory  
- GPU optimization for cost and performance  

---

### 6. Automation Engineering
- Build end-to-end AI agents  
- Replace manual workflows with automation  
- Use APIs, Python, and no-code tools  
- Deliver client-ready automation systems  

---

### 7. Solutions Architecture Expertise
- Build high-level system designs  
- Define system components:
  - data ingestion  
  - processing layer  
  - model layer  
  - API layer  
  - UI layer  
  - logging/monitoring  
- Use Docker + Kubernetes for scalability  
- Skill level: senior / team lead readiness  

---

title: # Course 00: on boarding for AI Accelarotor
text:
---

### 8. Online Presence & Brand
- LinkedIn:
  - authority positioning  
  - strong profile structure  
- GitHub:
  - clean repos  
  - documentation  
  - real demos  
- Portfolio:
  - project-based proof of skill  
- Content:
  - technical posts and project breakdowns  
- CV/Resume:
  - optimized for AI roles with measurable impact

## Production AI System

Build a complete AI application using:

- LangGraph
- MCP
- Agno
- Multimodal Components

### Features
- Agent Orchestration
- Tool Calling
- External Integrations
- Context Management
- Production Deployment

# Learning Outcomes

By the end of this course you will be able to:

- Build production-ready AI agents using LangGraph
- Design and implement MCP servers and clients
- Integrate MCP with LangChain, ReAct, and Claude
- Develop AI applications using Agno
- Build multimodal AI systems
- Deploy scalable AI workflows in production environments

title: CURRICULUM VITAE
text:
CURRICULUM VITAE
Jihad Lamaa
Chiah, Beirut, Lebanon | +961-70-817392 | ji190579@gmail.com
Date of Birth: May 19, 1979
Marital Status: Single

Experience Summary

Software Engineer with 20 years of experience in core banking, retail, and trade finance technology. Expertise spans software enhancements, system modernization, and architectural transformation.
- Enhancements & Maintenance: Enhanced and maintained core banking modules (PowerBuilder desktop applications) on Oracle and Sybase environments.
- Modernization & Migration: Converted retail and trade finance applications from PowerBuilder to Java web applications using the MVC 2 pattern (Struts2, Spring, MyBatis3).
- Architecture Transformation: Initiated and led the transformation of trade finance applications from Java web architecture to a microservices architecture.

Personal Development

Over the past year, I have been actively learning AI fundamentals through theory and practice. I joined the AI Engineers Program by Lara Wehbi (https://www.skool.com/theaiengineers), covering machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI. I am currently developing a customized chatbot for inquiries using RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) technology. See my AI project on GitHub: https://github.com/ji190579/rag_llm_notes.git

Technical Summary

title: CURRICULUM VITAE
text:
Technical Summary

- Technologies & Industries: JEE, PowerBuilder, PowerScript, Spring Boot, Microservices
- Database Environments: Oracle (PL/SQL), Sybase 15 (queries & stored procedures)
- Web/Application Servers: Tomcat
- Programming Languages: Java/JEE, HTML, JSP, Ajax, JavaScript, CSS, XML, PowerScript (PowerBuilder), Python
- Frameworks: Struts2 (MVC), Spring, Spring Boot
- ORM: MyBatis3
- Operating Systems: Windows
- Version Control: Microsoft Visual SourceSafe (VSS), Subversion (SVN), Git

Career Shift Summary

June 2006 – 2021: Assistant Principal Software Engineer, Path Solutions, Mansourieh, Lebanon (http://www.path-solutions.com)
June 2021 – Present: Principal Software Engineer, Azentio (formerly Path Solutions), Mansourieh, Lebanon (https://www.azentio.com)

Professional Experience


Software Engineer, Path Solutions

June 2006 – April 2011
Project: Desktop Core Banking Applications
- Designed, developed, and maintained PowerBuilder desktop applications for over 80 clients, covering journal vouchers, bank reconciliation, statements, points/interest, trial balance, and end-of-day processing.
- Responsible for designing objects and functional elements, developing and modifying source code for new applications and modifications.
- Fixed bugs and developed enhancements in both application code and stored procedures.
- Involved in all phases of SDLC: requirement gathering, design, analysis, and coding.

Senior Java Software Engineer, Path Solutions

title: CURRICULUM VITAE
text:
Senior Java Software Engineer, Path Solutions

2011 – 2014
Project: Web Retail Banking Applications
- Converted and refactored parts in in Banking Retail Applications (Front Office Management, Account Creation, Journal Voucher) from PowerBuilder to Java Client-Server (JEE), applying MVC using Struts2, Spring, and MyBatis3.
- Developed scripts and PL/SQL programs for data integration.
- Implemented server-side tasks in Java, integrating Spring DAO and MyBatis, and developing Struts action classes.

Associate Principal Java Software Engineer, Path Solutions

September 2014 – 2021
Project: Web and Desktop Trade Finance Applications
- Led transformation of Trade Finance applications (Letter of Credit, Guarantee, Bills for Collection) from PowerBuilder to Java client-server (JEE), applying MVC with Struts2, Spring, and MyBatis3.
- Created business scenarios to ensure successful migration from PowerBuilder to Java web apps.
- Provided training, conducted code reviews, and supported team members.
- Aligned business requirements with development teams; analyzed, documented, and planned technical solutions.
- Resolved production and critical issues as part of the escalation team.
- Participated in estimation sessions to align technical and QA expectations.

Principal Java Software Engineer, Azentio (formerly Path Solutions)

title: CURRICULUM VITAE
text:
Principal Java Software Engineer, Azentio (formerly Path Solutions)

September 2021 – Present
Project: Web Trade Finance Application
- Lead analysis, implementation, and development of the strategic roadmap for the Trade Finance application as Product Manager/Owner.
- Migrated SWIFT payment messages (MT103, MT202) to ISO 20022 (MX).
- Initiated transformation of trade finance application from Struts2-based Java web app to microservices architecture, including API Gateway, Eureka, Zipkin, Kafka, Keycloak, JFrog, and deployment with Podman/Docker.
